rulings.Case Title: MAYNARD GARCIA et al VS. PEAK ATTRACTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT REBOUND UNLIMITED, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY'S E-MAIL TO THE COURT. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted. The written subcontract agreement between Paul Ryan Associates (PRA) and American Terrazzo Co. (ATC) contains a duty to indemnify, which includes the duty to defend under CC § 2778(4) and Crawford v Weather Shield (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541. PRA has shown that the claims at issue here are potentially covered and that it tendered its defense to ATC. ATC fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Statutes in effect as of January 2006 (e.g. CC § 2778(a)) do not eviscerate the impact of Crawford because they do not obtain other than when the underlying acts are claimed to be the 'sole' negligence of the indemnitee; here acts of entities other than the indemnitee are also cited, including acts in connection with the exterior and interior Terrazzo. E.g., Normandie complaint 8 (complaint of workmanship of PRA's subcontractors). ATC's suggestion that allegations in cross complaints and assertions in discovery (such as Normandie's interrogatory responses, which contrary to ATC's argument, are not used to establish the truth of the matter and as to which therefore objections are overruled) do not prove ATC's negligence are rejected, because the issue here is the state of the allegations. PRA presented evidence of the tender of defense (Fact 17), as well as through the filing of this lawsuit. The difference in wording of the indemnification language as between the agreement and the tender letter is, for this purpose, immaterial. The tender issue is therefore not a reasonable basis to deny the motion for summary adjudication. Objections to evidence submitted by ATC are overruled. The Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: TIFFANY RUBY VS. STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents And Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 22, 2012 line 2 - PLAINTIFF TIFFANY RUBY's Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents And Further Responses. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the opposition papers pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MUHAWIEH VS. SENAIT AFEWERK, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA NICKS FOODS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANTS SENAIT AFEWERK and ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. CCP §473.5 is not applicable to the instant facts, as it pertains only when service is proper but there is excusable neglect. Defendants have failed to show their lack of actual knowledge is not due to inexcusable neglect or avoidance of service. Instead, defendants contend there was no service at all, and that the proofs of service were fraudulently created. See e.g. Brown & Weil, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial § 5:435 (2010). Moving parties have not sustained their proof on the issue, indeed submitting documents (the Bible school sign out sheet) which appears to have been materially altered to reflect a time consistent with defendants' current position. Furthermore, defendants provide nothing in support of the requirement that they have a "meritorious defense," nor did they proceed with diligence. Id. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS VS. LILLIAN BAU LIN FAN AKA LILLIAN CHOU, AKA LILLIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANTS DIMITRIOS STAVRAKIS and PATRICIA MOLINA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. How the contract should be interpreted and whether it is unenforceable is a factual question not properly decided on demurrer. The Second Amended Cross-Complaint is properly pleaded and it does not appear from the face of the Second Amended Cross-Complaint that Cross-Complainant is attempting to waive its statutory obligation to conduct a competent inspection, its fiduciary duty, its liability for fraud, or its liability for its own wrongdoing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 22, 2012 line 12 - PLAINTIFF WENDY DUJARDIN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $14,700. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LEPAKSHI NADH JILAKARA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 12, PLAINTIFF LEPAKSHI JILAKARA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to the second, third, fourth, fifth, eighth, ninth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, fourteenth and seventeenth affirmative defenses and denied as to the second cause of action and sixth, seventh, fourteenth and sixteenth affirmative defenses. City and County of San Francisco ("CCSF") does not oppose summary adjudication of the second, third, fourth, fifth, eighth, ninth, tenth, eleventh, thirteenth, fourteenth and seventeenth affirmative defenses. There are triable issues of fact whether Mr. Jilakara's action was a provoking act and whether there was an assumption of risk or comparative negligence. Mr. Jilakara does not cite to any legal authority to support his argument against the trespass affirmative defense. CCSF does not provide any evidence to support its contention that facts essential to justify its opposition to summary adjudication of the mitigation affirmative defense may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, then be presented. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ASSET CAPITAL RECOVERY GROUP, LLC, A LIMITED Q VS. REY G STOTOMAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted, Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 22, 2012 line 10 - PLAINTIFF ASSET CAPITAL RECOVERY GROUP, LLC, A LIMITED's Motion For Order To Deem Matters Admitted, Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party may re-file motion but must give new notice of motion and hearing per CCP §§1005 et seq. and comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HONGIK KIM VS. JOON ANG DAILY NEWS SF INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTS SANG KIM, YONG HAN, DAVID KIM, JUNEBUM PARK'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT - The motion is denied. While Defendant demonstrates that the slander cause of action may be a SLAPP action within the meaning of CCP 425.16, Plaintiff has demonstrated a reasonable possibility of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff's declaration raises factual issues regarding whether (1) the prelitigation statements related to litigation that was contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration and (2) defendants acted with malice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: WHITE OAK HOLDINGS, LLC et al VS. PHILIP N. DUFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 22, 2012, Line 19, CROSS DEFENDANTS WHITE OAK HOLDINGS, LLC, WHITE OAK PARTNERS LLC, WHITE OAK FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC, WHITE OAK MERCHANT PARTNERS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - Overruled as to the first and fifth causes of action, and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second cause of action. Cross-Complainants have pled facts sufficient to allege causes of action under the operating agreement; moving party's suggestion (Memorandum of Points & Authorities at 6 line 21) that the cross complaint (XC) does not plead an enforceable contract is incorrect. Compare, e.g., XC at 28, 48. See also Del. C. § 18-101(7)(b). Cross-Complainants request leave to amend the allegations of the second cause of action, granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
